decisions.text: Replace or remediate the bookkeeper function due to repeated errors, inaccurate P&L reporting, and 930+ days of outstanding reconciliations undermining organizational credibility and fundraising capacity.
Establish formal job descriptions, performance review schedules, and decision-making authority matrices to create accountability mechanisms across the organization.

Rationalize cost structure by addressing underperforming personnel, realigning Director compensation to sustainable salary level, and renegotiating unsustainable subsidy relationships with Quantum Americas.

Delegate transactional administrative work from Director to appropriate staff, clarify organizational roles and decision authority across interconnected entities (PZC, Quantum Americas, other Zen organizations).

actionItems.text: Resolve 930+ days of outstanding year-end financial reconciliation backlog and implement system-generated reporting to replace manual P&L creation, enabling timely 990 filing and grant applications.
Conduct formal performance review and capability assessment of bookkeeper (Amy) with documented performance expectations and measurable KPIs; determine fitness for role or execute personnel change.
Increase Director compensation from $40/hour to sustainable salary level reflecting 30+ hours/week responsibility for strategic planning, administration, and fundraising; address retention risk.
Create formal written job descriptions with clear responsibilities, decision authority, and performance metrics for all staff positions, establishing documented accountability framework.
Renegotiate Quantum Americas subsidy relationship and Sangha weekend pricing model; address unpaid invoices from 2019 and establish sustainable cost structure for programming.

commitments.text: Finance committee committed to establishing clear financial controls, accurate P&L reporting, and proper reconciliation procedures to ensure organizational credibility for fundraising and compliance.

Leadership committed to implementing governance infrastructure including formal decision-making authority matrices and clear escalation paths to reduce triangulation and improve organizational transparency.

Organization committed to building administrative infrastructure through documented processes, checklists, and system automation to reduce dependency on individual key persons and improve sustainability.

risks.text: Continued financial mismanagement and bookkeeping errors compromising organizational credibility, preventing timely 990 filing, blocking grant applications, and eroding donor trust in fundraising capacity.
risks.mitigation: Replace bookkeeper immediately; implement system-generated reporting; resolve reconciliation backlog; establish finance committee oversight and monthly reporting review.

risks.text: Director burnout and turnover due to unsustainable 30+ hour/week workload, underpaid compensation ($40/hour), and lack of administrative support; threatens institutional knowledge and strategic continuity.
risks.mitigation: Increase Director salary to sustainable level; hire qualified administrative support; delegate transactional work; establish clear role boundaries and decision authority.

risks.text: Legal and compliance risks from lack of formal policies, documented decision-making, and performance management procedures; potential labor law violations and audit exposure.
risks.mitigation: Create formal job descriptions and performance review schedule; document personnel decisions with finance committee consultation; establish written organizational policies.

risks.text: Loss of organizational knowledge and operational paralysis if key individuals depart without succession planning or institutional documentation; threatens long-term 50-year sustainability goals.
risks.mitigation: Develop succession plans for critical roles; create knowledge transfer and documentation systems; build administrative infrastructure reducing dependency on key persons.
